question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who controls or poisons the mirror node REST endpoint (cfg.mirrorRest) return a crafted nodesEnvelope JSON with all serviceEndpoints pointing to unreachable IPs, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build a network map that permanently partitions the pinger from all consensus nodes, resulting in all submitWithRetry() calls failing indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker perform DNS poisoning against the domain resolved from cfg.mirrorRest to redirect buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to an attacker-controlled server, injecting a fake nodesEnvelope with adversarial node endpoints, causing the pinger to operate on a fully attacker-controlled network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST server to return an empty Nodes slice in nodesEnvelope, so that hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) receives an empty map, causing the pinger to be partitioned from all consensus nodes with no error surfaced to the operator?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who sits on-path between the pinger and the mirror node REST endpoint inject a crafted HTTP response with a nodeEntry containing a GrpcProxyEndpoint pointing to an attacker-controlled host, causing all SDK gRPC traffic to be routed through the attacker and creating a controlled network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints containing IPAddressV4 values set to loopback (127.0.0.1) or link-local addresses, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build a network map that routes all consensus traffic to non-routable addresses, inducing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can delay or drop gRPC responses from consensus nodes cause submitWithRetry() to exhaust all cfg.maxRetries attempts with exponential backoff, resulting in the pinger logging 'all attempts failed' every tick and creating a sustained effective network partition that is never self-healing?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can force repeated transfer failures cause the backoff() function's bit-shift calculation (base * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1))) to overflow for large attempt values, producing a zero or negative sleep duration that causes a tight retry loop, exhausting CPU and amplifying network traffic during a partition event?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can delay all consensus node responses beyond the SDK's internal timeout cause the heartbeat goroutine's 15-second ticker to continue writing /tmp/alive while submitWithRetry() silently fails every tick, masking a complete network partition from the liveness probe and preventing automated recovery?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can exhaust the /tmp filesystem (e.g., via a co-located container or shared volume) prevent os.WriteFile('/tmp/alive', ...) from succeeding, causing the liveness exec probe to fail and triggering a pod restart that interrupts the pinger's network partition detection loop?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with a non-nil Links.Next pointer in an infinite pagination loop, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to make unbounded HTTP requests, exhausting connection pool resources and inducing a self-inflicted network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return an extremely large JSON body (e.g., millions of nodeEntry records), causing json.Unmarshal in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to exhaust heap memory, crashing the pinger process and causing a network partition by removing the pinger from the monitoring topology?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can influence the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK environment variable to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to an attacker-controlled URL cause buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to fetch a crafted node list, partitioning the pinger from the legitimate Hiero network entirely?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return nodeEntry records with Port values set to 0 or 65535, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build connections to invalid ports, inducing a network partition for all consensus node communication?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker perform an HTTP response splitting or header injection attack against the mirror node REST endpoint to cause fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to parse a malformed nodesEnvelope, resulting in an empty or corrupted network map that partitions the pinger from consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can selectively drop gRPC packets to consensus nodes cause cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) to hang until the SDK's internal deadline, then cause resp.GetReceipt(client) to also timeout, doubling the effective latency per attempt and causing all cfg.maxRetries attempts to be consumed before the context deadline, creating a sustained partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can send SIGTERM to the pinger process (e.g., via a shared Kubernetes namespace or container escape) trigger the signal.NotifyContext() cancellation, causing the pinger to shut down and stop monitoring network health, effectively hiding a network partition from operators?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodeEntry with a NodeAccountID that does not parse as a valid hiero.AccountID, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to silently skip or panic on that entry, reducing the effective network map and creating a partial network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to respond with HTTP 503 or 429 on every attempt, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to exhaust all mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries with exponential backoff and return an error, preventing newClient() from initializing and causing a startup-time network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to delay responses beyond cfg.mirrorNodeClientTimeout on every attempt, causing all fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() calls to return timeout errors, exhausting retries and preventing the pinger from ever building a valid network map?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can inject RST packets into the TCP connection between the pinger and consensus nodes cause cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) to return a connection-reset error on every attempt, exhausting all cfg.maxRetries retries and causing the pinger to log 'all attempts failed' every tick without triggering any automated recovery?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause repeated transfer failures at every ticker.C tick cause the pinger's main loop to log errors indefinitely without any circuit-breaker or alerting mechanism, allowing a network partition to persist undetected for an unbounded duration?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope where all nodeEntry.ServiceEndpoints have empty DomainName and empty IPAddressV4, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build a network map with unresolvable endpoints, inducing a complete network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker perform a BGP hijack against the IP prefix of the mirror node REST endpoint, redirecting buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() requests to an attacker-controlled server that returns a crafted nodesEnvelope with adversarial node IPs, causing the pinger to operate on a partitioned network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the Hiero network to return BUSY or PLATFORM_NOT_ACTIVE status codes on every transaction cause submitWithRetry() to exhaust all retries and return 'all attempts failed', creating an effective network partition from the pinger's perspective without any actual network-layer disruption?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can force cfg.maxRetries to be set to a very large value (e.g., via environment variable HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES) cause the backoff() function to compute durations that, when summed across all attempts, far exceed the ticker interval, causing the retry loop to block the main goroutine and miss subsequent ticks, amplifying the impact of a network partition?
